name: Liquid cooling & rack-scale thermal
severityNote: Capacity ramping fast; spec compliance is the gate
description: Direct-to-chip cold plates, coolant distribution units (CDUs), and rack manifolds are mandatory for NVL72-class racks running >100 kW per rack. The market is supply-constrained on engineering hours and validation labs more than on materials; lead times are falling but qualification cycles per hyperscaler are long.
stackPosition: Cold plate (copper micro-channel, on GPU/CPU) → manifold + quick-disconnect → in-rack distribution → CDU (rack or row level) → secondary loop → facility chilled water → cooling tower OR dry cooler. Materials are commoditized; the binding constraint is integration engineering at the rack OEM (Foxconn, Quanta, Wiwynn, Supermicro) and the validation labs at hyperscalers.

supplyStructure.name: Asetek + Coolerator + GRC + Submer
supplyStructure.note: Asetek taken private 2024; mostly small-cap / private names

indicators.name: Vertiv liquid cooling order book and book-to-bill
Delta Electronics monthly revenue and segment commentary
Schneider Electric Secure Power segment and Motivair integration
nVent and Modine datacenter cooling segment results
NVIDIA NVL72 / NVL576 deployment milestones at hyperscalers

companies.name: Vertiv
companies.note: Largest pure-play datacenter infrastructure; highest beta to AI rack ramp

companies.name: Schneider Electric
companies.note: Motivair acquisition brings DLC IP in-house; European listing

companies.name: Delta Electronics
companies.note: Power + cooling combined; integrated rack delivery model

companies.name: nVent Electric
companies.note: Smaller share but pure-play DLC + enclosures

companies.name: Modine Manufacturing
companies.note: Edge / smaller datacenter HVAC; less pure AI mix

companies.name: Asetek
companies.note: Workstation DLC heritage; taken private by Solar III Holdings 2024

companies.name: Submer
companies.note: Single-phase immersion cooling; gaining sovereign-AI datacenter deployments in EU/MENA

companies.name: JetCool Technologies
companies.note: Microconvective DLC at chip level; commercial DLC for HPE / Supermicro

companies.name: Iceotope
companies.note: Chassis-level precision immersion; edge + AI deployments

companies.name: ZutaCore
companies.note: HyperCool waterless two-phase DLC; Equinix + Mizuno deployments

insight: Liquid cooling is rapidly moving from constrained to competitive — the right framing is not 'who has capacity' but 'who has hyperscaler qualification at scale.' Vertiv is the cleanest US-listed expression with the highest AI-rack mix; Schneider's Motivair integration is the upside catalyst over the next 18 months. The widow-maker trade is the small-cap DLC pure plays (Asetek, Coolerator-class) that look mispriced on technology but lose on relationship and execution. Watch immersion cooling closely — the technical advantages are real but hyperscaler adoption has been slower than industry voices suggest.

debates.question: Does direct-to-chip liquid cooling penetrate >60% of new AI builds by end of 2026?
debates.currentBelief: Likely — NVL72 hard requirement plus Meta + Microsoft DLC roadmap commitments

debates.question: Does immersion cooling reach >10% share of new AI deployments by 2027?
debates.currentBelief: Unlikely — qualification cycle longer, hyperscaler ops resistance persistent
